mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database designed for semantic search and data retrieval, combining vector similarity with structured filtering. It is ideal for applications like recommendation systems, chatbots, and content classification. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Weaviate Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Weaviate Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Weaviate using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add weaviate https://weaviate.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Weaviate Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Weaviate CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Weaviate to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviate.yaml

```

> This command applies the Weaviate CRDs.

steps.title: Install Weaviate using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Weaviate in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --create-namespace --version 1.28.0

```

> Replace '1.28.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Create Weaviate instance
steps.description:
This step creates an instance of Weaviate by applying a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate-instance
  namespace: weaviate
spec:
  replication:
    enabled: true
EOF

```

> This command creates a Weaviate instance with replication enabled.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate-instance
  namespace: weaviate
spec:
  replication:
    enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Weaviate installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Weaviate pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate

```

> This command lists all pods in the Weaviate namespace.
> This command checks the status of the Weaviate instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate
uninstall.description:
This step removes Weaviate and its associated resources from the Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall weaviate --namespace weaviate

kubectl delete namespace weaviate

```

> This command uninstalls the Weaviate Helm release.
> This command deletes the Weaviate namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Weaviate to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

helm upgrade weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --version 1.28.1

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> Replace '1.28.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n weaviate' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the Helm chart values.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pending' status for pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources available in the cluster to schedule the Weaviate pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) to accommodate Weaviate.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Weaviate cannot connect to the configured database backend.

**Fix:**
Check the database connection settings in the Weaviate configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new vectorization modules and improved RBAC features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.27, ensure to review the new RBAC configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Weaviate instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n weaviate'.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate-instance
  namespace: weaviate
spec:
  replication:
    enabled: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
